didimpute 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method

Source: R/standard_errors.R
dot-compute_df_a.Rd

Mirrors the pyhdfe algorithm.degrees logic (used with cluster_ids): an FE is dropped if it is nested within (i.e., its levels are a refinement of) any cluster group. The remaining FEs contribute their unique level counts, minus the number of connected components across pairs (pairwise method).

Usage

.compute_df_a(dt, fe, cluster)

Arguments

dt

data.table restricted to the untreated subsample

fe

character vector of FE column names

cluster

name of cluster column

Value

integer absorbed degrees of freedom

Details

For the standard case (fe = c(i, t), cluster = i): the i-FE is nested within the i-cluster, so only the t-FE contributes => df_a = uniqueN(t).

On this page
